key_departures.impact: Led the Cowboys in sacks in 2025 with 8.5 sacks and 40 pressures on a one-year deal signed mid-September. New DC Christian Parker's scheme shift appears to be steering Dallas toward other options. Massive void at the top of the EDGE depth chart.
Rotational edge rusher who had 3.0 sacks and 30 pressures in 2025 after a 10.5-sack season in Washington. Cowboys not expected to bring him back. Loss of rotational depth compounds the EDGE need.

Seven-year Cowboys veteran and 75-game starter released February 2 to free up $6.5M in cap space. New DC Christian Parker moving toward more athletic safeties. Created a starting safety hole that the team addressed via free agency.

Waived February 2026 after being acquired at the trade deadline from the Bengals for a 7th-round pick. Saved the Cowboys $6.5M. Struggled in Dallas with just one start in seven games. Departure deepens an already thin LB room.
Veteran special teamer and depth CB. Departure is minimal impact on the starting roster but thins special teams depth.

signings.trade_compensation: Cowboys sent 2027 4th-round pick to Green Bay

contract.notes: Inherited contract: $19.5M base salary in 2026, $21M base salary in 2027 — ZERO guaranteed money remaining per OverTheCap. Cowboys expected to attempt restructure. Cap hit for 2026: ~$19.5M.

signings.analysis: Pro Bowl pass rusher in 2024 with 7.5 sacks in both 2024 and 2025, 46.5 career sacks. Acquired for only a 2027 4th-round pick — a bargain in draft capital terms. However, Gary has zero guaranteed money left and was considered a Packers cut candidate. His 2025 production declined in the second half of the season, and new DC Parker's scheme may or may not maximize him. The $19.5M cap hit in 2026 is significant for a team already over the cap. Starter-caliber addition, but the Cowboys explicitly need more EDGE help beyond Gary — Donovan Ezeiruaku (hip surgery) will miss OTAs/minicamp and Clowney is gone. EDGE is partially addressed, not resolved.

contract.notes: Base deal is 3yr/$33M per ESPN/NFL Network; max value is $36M per Ian Rapoport/Jane Slater. Guarantee details not yet fully disclosed; max value implies incentive-based upside. Spotrac lists AAV at $12M on $36M structure.

signings.analysis: Thompson, 27, started 87 career games over seven seasons in Arizona and has 9 INTs, 37 PBUs and 578 career tackles. He reunites with Cowboys secondary coach Ryan Smith, who coached him in Arizona. He pairs with re-structured Malik Hooker to give Dallas a legitimate starting safety tandem. The 3-year deal at $11-12M AAV is mid-tier starter money for S — appropriate for his profile. Three-year commitment with clear starter intent. Safety need is fully addressed; DAL may still add depth/developmental S in late rounds but it is no longer a premium need.

contract.notes: 2 void years included for cap purposes. 2026 cap hit: $3.95M. $6M signing bonus. 2026 and 2027 base salaries ($1.75M and $6.25M respectively) are fully guaranteed. Easy out in 2028.

signings.analysis: Williams was the Cowboys' pending free agent RB coming off a career year: 1,201 rushing yards, 11 TDs, 35 catches. Dallas locked him up pre-free agency on Feb. 22 for $24M/3yr with $16M guaranteed — well below the market ceiling (Kenneth Walker III got $43M/3yr). With 2 years of real guaranteed money and a clear starting role, RB is fully addressed. The team-friendly structure gives Dallas an out in 2028. No RB need at any tier.

contract.notes: Guarantee details not disclosed; one-year deal at $3M total per NFL Network/CBS Sports.

signings.analysis: Former 2nd-round pick (56th overall, 2022) returning from torn ACL/MCL that cost him the entire 2024 season. In 2025 he played 17 games (5 starts) but tallied only 1.0 sack and posted a dismal 8.7% pass-rush win rate. One-year prove-it deal at depth-tier money. Does not solve the EDGE starter need — merely adds rotational depth. EDGE remains a critical team need. With Clowney and Fowler gone and Ezeiruaku injured, the Cowboys still need a legitimate starter-quality EDGE via free agency or the draft.

contract.notes: Non-exclusive franchise tag at $27.3M fully guaranteed for 2026. Other teams may negotiate with Pickens; Cowboys have 5 days to match any offer sheet. If Cowboys decline to match, they receive two 1st-round picks.

signings.analysis: Pickens was a Pro Bowl selection in 2025. The non-exclusive tag buys time to negotiate a long-term deal while retaining rights. WR is not a need so long as Pickens remains tagged — Dallas has CeeDee Lamb ($34M AAV) and Pickens locked in at WR1/WR2. The tag does NOT resolve the long-term WR2 question (will need a multi-year deal) but eliminates it as a 2026 draft-day need.

contract.notes: Second-round RFA tender. Cowboys and Aubrey working toward a long-term extension expected to make him highest-paid kicker in NFL history.

signings.analysis: Retained via 2nd-round tender. No impact on positional needs — K was never a need. Long-term extension discussions ongoing.

contract.notes: Second-round RFA tender protects Cowboys' interior line depth.

signings.analysis: RFA tender keeps an interior lineman in the fold. One-year tender is not a long-term IOL solution. The Cowboys still have Brock Hoffman questions outstanding. IOL need is not meaningfully addressed by a tender alone.

contract.notes: Separate deal equal to ERFA number ($1.07M) with injury split added for team protection per ESPN's Todd Archer.

signings.analysis: ERFA-level deal to retain a young depth CB. No impact on positional needs.
Depth CB retained at minimum-level money. No impact on starting CB needs.

contract_restructures_and_cap_moves.details: Restructured contract now includes $22M in fully guaranteed money, creating approximately $13M in 2026 cap space.
contract_restructures_and_cap_moves.impact: Retains starting RT; effectively resolves OT as a near-term need on the right side. Cowboys still face LT uncertainty long-term.

contract_restructures_and_cap_moves.details: Contract restructured, freeing approximately $2M in 2026 cap space. Hooker retained as starting safety alongside new addition Jalen Thompson.
contract_restructures_and_cap_moves.impact: Confirms Hooker is in the 2026 plan. Pairs with Thompson to form the starting safety tandem.

contract_restructures_and_cap_moves.details: Cowboys waived DT Winfrey in early March. Appeared in only one game in 2025.
contract_restructures_and_cap_moves.impact: Negligible — was a fringe roster player.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Rashan Gary (trade, starter-caliber) and Sam Williams (re-sign, depth) added, but Clowney (8.5 sacks) and Fowler (3.0 sacks) both departed. Ezeiruaku missing all OTAs/minicamp with hip surgery. Gary has zero guaranteed money and declining production second-half of 2025. The net EDGE room is still critically thin — Gary + a recovering Ezeiruaku + a 1-sack Williams is not enough. EDGE remains the #1 draft priority and should still be targeted in early rounds, potentially with one of Dallas's two top-12 picks.

Jalen Thompson signing (3yr/$33-36M, clear starter) paired with restructured Malik Hooker gives Dallas a legitimate starting tandem. Thompson has started 87 career games and reunites with Cowboys secondary coach Ryan Smith. Safety is no longer a premium need. Drop to tier 4 — late-round developmental depth only.
Javonte Williams re-signed on 3yr/$24M with $16M guaranteed off a career year (1,201 yards, 11 TDs). Team-friendly deal with easy 2028 out. RB is fully addressed — remove from all need tiers.

George Pickens retained via franchise tag ($27.3M guaranteed for 2026). Combined with CeeDee Lamb, Dallas has a legitimate WR1/WR2 pairing. WR is not a draft need in 2026. Long-term extension talks will be the key offseason storyline but do not affect 2026 draft needs.

Logan Wilson waived (Feb. 2026), no LB added in free agency. Cowboys are left with DeMarvion Overshown (injury-prone), Shemar James (raw rookie), and Marist Liufau (transitioning to OLB in new 3-4 scheme). The LB room is one of the thinnest in the league entering the draft. Escalate to tier 1 co-equal with EDGE. Dallas needs a starter-quality off-ball linebacker urgently.

Terence Steele restructured ($22M guaranteed) and retained at RT. Right tackle is stable. Left tackle remains a concern — Cowboys acknowledge LT is a long-term question with limited 2026 FA options. Drop to tier 3: not an emergency, but still worth addressing with a mid-round pick or developmental target.

T.J. Bass retained on 2nd-round RFA tender, but only for 2026. Brock Hoffman's future is uncertain. The Cowboys' IOL depth beyond Tyler Smith (LG) is thin. Tier stays at 2 — interior line is a genuine mid-round draft priority.

No notable CB additions or losses that meaningfully change the need picture. Trevon Diggs (declining/injury-prone) remains a potential cut candidate. DaRon Bland returning from injury. CB need unchanged — Dallas still needs a capable starter opposite Bland if Diggs is released. Tier holds at 2.

Quinnen Williams, Kenny Clark, and Osa Odighizuwa remain on roster — three solid IDL starters. Clark's $21.5M cap hit with no guarantees is a potential cap casualty. If Clark is moved, IDL escalates. For now, tier holds at 3 — depth and contingency planning only.

key_additions.headline: Pro Bowl pass rusher (7.5 sacks in each of the past two seasons, 46.5 career) acquired for only a 4th-round pick to anchor a rebuild of Dallas's depleted EDGE room
87-career-start safety reunites with Cowboys secondary coach Ryan Smith to form a legitimate starting tandem with Malik Hooker
Cowboys lock up their 1,201-yard, 11-TD breakout back before free agency on a team-friendly deal well below market rate
Pro Bowl wideout retained on non-exclusive franchise tag while Cowboys pursue long-term extension with their electric WR2 alongside CeeDee Lamb
Former 2nd-round pick gets one more shot to prove himself as rotational EDGE depth after returning from torn ACL with limited production in 2025

key_losses.headline: Cowboys' best defender in 2025 (8.5 sacks, 40 pressures) hits UFA market as scheme shift under new DC Christian Parker makes his return unlikely
Seven-year Cowboys starter released as new DC Parker moves toward more athletic secondary pieces — creating the hole Jalen Thompson was signed to fill
Deadline trade acquisition waived just months after arriving, deepening Dallas's already thin linebacker room under new 3-4 scheme
Rotational pass rusher (3.0 sacks, 30 pressures in 2025) expected to depart, compounding the EDGE need alongside Clowney's exit

picks_traded_away: 2027 4th-round pick to Green Bay for Rashan Gary
draft_capital_notes.notes: Cowboys own two 1st-round picks in 2026 thanks to the Micah Parsons trade. With EDGE and LB both at tier 1 need and two premium selections in hand, expect both early picks to address defensive premium positions. The comp 5th-round picks were awarded for compensatory free agents lost (DeMarcus Lawrence, Jourdan Lewis, Rico Dowdle, Chauncey Golston, Chuma Edoga, Brandin Cooks).

confidence_notes: All signings confirmed by NFL Network, ESPN, CBS Sports, Pro Football Rumors, and/or official team announcements. Contract figures sourced from OverTheCap.com and Spotrac.com. Gary's contract figures (2yr/$40.5M remaining, zero guaranteed) confirmed by OverTheCap/CBS Sports. Thompson deal reported as 3yr/$33M base by ESPN's Todd Archer and 3yr/$36M max by Ian Rapoport — using $33M confirmed base with $36M max noted. Guarantee details on Thompson TBD in final contract language.
